tracks.meaning: to hit or deliver a blow to someone or something with force
in baseball, a pitch that the batter does not swing at and that is within the strike zone
a legal procedure to remove a portion of evidence or testimony from the court record
a count against a player or team in sports, accumulating toward disqualification
a coordinated stoppage of work by employees to protest working conditions or seek better terms
a targeted attack on a specific location or enemy position
the direction of the line formed by the intersection of a tilted rock layer with a horizontal plane
a rich vein or deposit of ore discovered suddenly
the fixed price at which an option contract can be exercised
to lower or remove a sail or flag
the sudden sounding of a clock or bell to mark the hour
a memorable phrase or idea that catches attention or impresses an audience
to impress someone or make a favourable impression
to assume or move into a particular posture or attitude
knocking down all ten pins with a single ball in bowling
